transitions.note: Peer review by Claude (self-review after user called out miscalibrated priors)
Build the full A1+A2 stack now. A1 (product-improvement AI) reads telemetry, writes recommendations,

question: Should resume-resume build a multi-level autonomous AI management stack (A1 = product-improvement AI reads telemetry, A2 = process-management AI watches A1 and proposes methodology changes, human manages A2), or a lighter variant where the human manually performs A2's role for a calibration period, or defer entirely?
context: resume-resume now has telemetry (per-MCP-call JSONL) and self_* introspection tools. User wants highest-leverage human role: managing a meta-AI that manages a product-improvement AI. Claude (advisor) expressed concerns: (1) A1's output must auto-apply for A2 to have real work, (2) A2's feedback loop is weeks long, (3) small surface area (~2k LOC, 23 tools), (4) infinite regress justification, (5) loss of product intuition. User counter: AI engineer wants to learn by building. Claude suggested: be A2 manually for 2 months, then automate once ground truth exists. User invoked trilogy (research.md earns decisions with evidence). Current state: recommendations.py + evaluator.py (= A1) already scaffolded uncommitted on working tree.
